title: Customer Service Disputes
description: Handling complaints professionally: listening, apologizing, offering a solution.
summary_pt_br: Atendimento: 'I understand your frustration', 'I'm sorry', 'Let me see', 'Would a refund work?'

scenes.subtitle_en: Handling complaints professionally: listening, apologizing, offering a solution.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Atendimento: 'I understand your frustration', 'I'm sorry', 'Let me see', 'Would a refund work?'

scenes.text_en: Handling complaints professionally: listening, apologizing, offering a solution. Today's focus: customer service disputes. I am so sorry this happened on your visit.
scenes.text_pt: Atendimento: 'I understand your frustration', 'I'm sorry', 'Let me see', 'Would a refund work?' Foco de hoje: customer service disputes. Me desculpa por isso ter acontecido.

scenes.title: Customer Service Disputes
scenes.body_en: Key phrases: 'I understand your frustration.', 'I'm sorry this happened.', 'Let me see what I can do.', 'Would a refund work for you?'
scenes.body_pt: Atendimento: 'I understand your frustration', 'I'm sorry', 'Let me see', 'Would a refund work?'

examples.en: I am so sorry this happened on your visit.
examples.pt: Me desculpa por isso ter acontecido.

examples.en: Let me see what I can do to make it right.
Would a full refund work for you?

exercise.question: Complete: "I am so sorry this ___ on your visit."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "I am so sorry this happened on your visit."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Me desculpa por isso ter acontecido.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Let me see what I can do to make it right.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "Let me see what I can do to make it right."
Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Customer Service Disputes — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre customer service disputes. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: My food was cold and the order took too long!

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I am so sorry this happened today.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I've never had such bad service here!

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I want my money back and an explanation!

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Let me see what I can do right now.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: And the waiter was rude when I complained.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I will speak with him personally, I promise.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: A full refund plus a voucher for next time.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Thank you. That actually changes how I feel.

scenes.title: A difficult Tuesday lunch
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the complaint and how Rafa handled it step by step. Use at least three customer service phrases.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: A regular lunchtime at Tropical Bites turned tense when a loyal customer received cold food.

paragraphs.speaker: Customer
paragraphs.en: This is the third time something has gone wrong this month. I'm exhausted.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: I am genuinely sorry this happened. Your time matters as much as your meal.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: Let me offer a full refund and a voucher for your next visit, on me.

paragraphs.speaker: Customer
paragraphs.en: You just turned a terrible experience into a reason to come back.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Good customer service, Rafa believed, lives in how you handle the worst moments — never the best ones.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Customer Service Disputes". Handling complaints professionally: listening, apologizing, offering a solution. I am so sorry this happened on your visit. Let me see what I can do to make it right. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Customer Service Disputes". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Customer Service Disputes".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: BBC 6 Minute English
sources.pt_hint: Podcast curto com transcrição — nível B1.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias e áudios em inglês adaptado.
